title: Lasagna with Turkey Sausage
description: From Barefoot Contessa Family Style — the centerpiece of Ina's Italian Night family dinner menu. A rich, layered lasagna with turkey sausage, creamy béchamel, and a slow-simmered tomato sauce. It's a true make-ahead masterpiece — assemble it the day before and bake when you need it.
instructions: Make the meat sauce: Heat the olive oil in a large (12-inch) skillet over medium heat. Add the turkey sausage and cook, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes. Add the onion and garlic and cook for 5 more minutes. Add the crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, basil, salt, and pepper. Simmer over low heat for 15 minutes.

Make the béchamel: Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the warm milk and cream. Cook, whisking constantly, until the sauce thickens, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in the Parmesan and nutmeg. Season with salt and pepper.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Spread a thin layer of meat sauce on the bottom of a 9×13-inch baking dish.

Layer the lasagna: Place 4 no-boil noodles over the sauce. Spread half the ricotta over the noodles. Spoon half the remaining meat sauce over the ricotta. Pour half the béchamel over the meat sauce. Sprinkle with ⅓ of the mozzarella. Repeat the layers: noodles, ricotta, meat sauce, béchamel, mozzarella. Finish with the remaining noodles, the last of the béchamel, the remaining mozzarella, and the Parmesan.

Cover tightly with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for another 15 minutes, until bubbling and the top is golden brown. Allow to rest for 10 minutes before cutting and serving.
tips: This is a perfect make-ahead dish — assemble the day before, refrigerate, and bake when needed (add 10 minutes to the covered baking time if coming from the refrigerator).
No-boil lasagna noodles work perfectly here and save a lot of time.
The béchamel is what makes this lasagna extraordinary — don't substitute jarred Alfredo sauce.
Source: Barefoot Contessa Family Style by Ina Garten (2002)
